strongly_clumped_D_negative.interpretation: Lineage-specific traits: intrinsic AMR genes and host-associated niches are phylogenetically conserved. blaA/vat(F) D=-17 confirms they are lineage markers, not horizontally acquired genes.

random_D_near_1.interpretation: Mobile AMR genes with active HGT: distributed randomly across the phylogeny, consistent with frequent horizontal transfer. Environmental niche (D=0.86) confirms any lineage can colonize environmental habitats.

overdispersed_D_above_1.interpretation: Convergent acquisition: genes appear in multiple independent lineages more often than random. Consistent with strong selection pressure driving parallel AMR acquisition events across the genus.

key_findings.central_dichotomy: Clear dichotomy between lineage-specific resistance (D=-17 to -5, p<0.001) and mobile/convergent resistance (D=0.87-1.27, p_random>0.17). This phylogenetic framework separates intrinsic from acquired AMR at genus scale.
